finding.contract_claim: Parameter `plumbing_result` is declared in the signature of build_scorecard(config, analysis_results, junk_results=None, dead_code_results=None, plumbing_result=None) (signature at lines 106-113) as an accepted input that callers may pass.
finding.observed_behavior: No call site ever passes `plumbing_result`: the only caller, run_audit in src/osoji/audit.py, invokes build_scorecard with config, analysis_results and junk_results only, so the parameter always takes its default None and the branches gated on it never execute.

finding.triage_reasoning: Reality: Same analysis as Claim 8. The only scanned caller never passes `plumbing_result`, so the branches gated on `plumbing_result is not None` (L238-248) are permanently dead. Significance: The backward-compat comment explains the history but per triage rules does not close the gap. Dead branches accumulate technical debt. The parameter is not exported. Actionability: Remove the `plumbing_result` parameter and the compat block at L238-248.
finding.suggested_fix: Remove the `plumbing_result` parameter from `build_scorecard` and delete the backward-compat block gated on `plumbing_result is not None`. Ensure no external callers pass this argument first.
